message.content: Look at comments at https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/pull/463, fix them then solve them.

[Let us know!](https://github.com/codecov/feedback/issues/255)","createdAt":"2026-03-23T10:23:23Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/pull/463#issuecomment-4109530608","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOREW6tc709CS9","author":{"login":"greptile-apps"},"authorAssociation":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"

Greptile Summary

This PR replaces the ~100-line hand-rolled HTML-to-text parser in `web_fetch.rs` with the `html2text` crate (0.16, MIT), fixing the multibyte panic (#420) that crashed on GBK/Big5-encoded pages and gaining correct decoding of all HTML entities. The approach is sound and the regression tests are well-targeted, but there are three issues that should be addressed before merging: - **Potential link footnote injection** — `html2text::from_read` with its default decorator may append `[n]: https://...` footnote blocks to the rendered text, injecting URLs that the old parser dropped entirely. This is a behavioural change that could pollute LLM context and is not covered by any test. - **Silent error handling** — `Err(_) => String::new()` silently discards any parse error, returning an empty string that is indistinguishable from a genuinely empty page. Per the project's logging guidelines, this should emit a `tracing::warn!` so operators can diagnose failures. - **`usize::MAX` as the width sentinel** — Passing `usize::MAX` to `html2text::from_read` relies on undocumented overflow-safe arithmetic in the library's table-layout code. A large explicit constant (`1_000_000`) or the `html2text::config::plain()` builder API is safer and more expressive. Additionally, the PR description lists `cargo fmt`, `clippy`, and the full `local-validate.sh` run as **Remaining** (unchecked). Per project convention these must pass before merge.

Confidence Score: 2/5

- Not safe to merge until link-footnote behaviour is verified, silent error handling is fixed, and the mandatory CI gates (fmt, clippy, local-validate.sh) are completed. - The core fix (replacing unsafe byte-indexing with a proper HTML5 parser) is correct and the regression tests are solid. However, the link-footnote injection risk is a real behavioural change with no test coverage, the silent error path contradicts project logging guidelines, and the three CI validation steps listed as "Remaining" in the PR description are blocking requirements per CLAUDE.md. - crates/tools/src/web_fetch.rs — specifically the `html_to_text` function (lines 240–251)

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | crates/tools/src/web_fetch.rs | Core change: replaces ~100-line hand-rolled HTML parser with `html2text::from_read`. Fixes the multibyte panic (#420) and improves entity handling, but introduces three concerns: `usize::MAX` as the width sentinel, silent error swallowing on parse failure, and potential link-footnote injection into the plain-text output. | | Cargo.toml | Adds `html2text = "0.16"` to workspace dependencies following the project convention of centralising versions in the root manifest. No issues. | | crates/tools/Cargo.toml | Adds `html2text = { workspace = true }` to the crate's dependency list, correctly referencing the workspace version. No issues. | | Cargo.lock | Lockfile updated to include `html2text 0.16.7` and its transitive deps (`html5ever 0.38.0`, `markup5ever`, `tendril`, `string_cache`, `web_atoms`). Dependency tree looks clean; no suspicious or unexpected transitive additions. |

Sequence Diagram

```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Caller as AgentTool::execute participant FU as fetch_url() participant EC as extract_content() participant H2T as html_to_text() participant LIB as html2text::from_read Caller->>FU: url, extract_mode, max_chars FU->>FU: SSRF check FU->>FU: HTTP GET (reqwest) FU->>EC: body, content_type, extract_mode EC->>EC: detect JSON / plain-text EC->>H2T: html string H2T->>LIB: html.as_bytes(), usize::MAX alt parse OK LIB-->>H2T: Ok(raw_text) H2T->>H2T: trim_end lines, join, trim H2T-->>EC: cleaned String else parse error LIB-->>H2T: Err(e) H2T-->>EC: String::new() ← error silently dropped end EC-->>FU: (content, mode) FU->>FU: truncate_at_char_boundary if needed FU-->>Caller: JSON { url, content, truncated, … } ``` Reviews (1): Last reviewed commit: ["fix(tools): replace hand-rolled html_to_..."](https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/commit/b61f7492affb076b1a11183e5789c22f85d0556b) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=26025724)","createdAt":"2026-03-23T10:41:13Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/moltis-org/moltis/pull/463#issuecomment-4109640893","viewerDidAuthor":false}],"reviews":[{"id":"PRR_kwDOREW6tc7t3Uc2","author":{"login":"chatgpt-codex-connector"},"authorAssociation":"NONE","body":" ### 💡 Codex Review Here are some automated review suggestions for this pull request. **Reviewed commit:** `b61f7492af`

Reviews are triggered when you - Open a pull request for review - Mark a draft as ready - Comment "@codex review". If Codex has suggestions, it will comment; otherwise it will react with 👍. Codex can also answer questions or update the PR. Try commenting "@codex address that feedback".

content.text:
Four review comments to fix:

1. **P1**: Silent error → log with `tracing::warn!`
2. **P2**: `usize::MAX` width → use builder API or large constant
3. **P2**: Error drops content → fall back to original HTML stripped
4. **P1**: Link footnotes injected → use plain config to suppress

Let me read the current code and check the html2text API.

input.command: cargo doc --document-private-items -p html2text 2>&1 | tail -5; grep -r "config::plain\|string_from_read\|TrivialDecorator\|PlainDecorator" ~/.cargo/registry/src/*/html2text-0.16*/src/ 2>/dev/null | head -30
input.description: Check html2text API for config::plain builder

content.text: Let me check what decorator `from_read` uses and how `TrivialDecorator` handles links.

content.content:
pub fn from_read_with_decorator<R, D>(input: R, width: usize, decorator: D) -> Result<String>
where
    R: io::Read,
    D: TextDecorator,
{
    config::with_decorator(decorator).string_from_read(input, width)
--
pub fn from_read<R>(input: R, width: usize) -> Result<String>
where
    R: io::Read,
{
    config::plain().string_from_read(input, width)
}
--
pub fn from_read_rich<R>(input: R, width: usize) -> Result<Vec<TaggedLine<Vec<RichAnnotation>>>>
where
    R: io::Read,
{
    config::rich().lines_from_read(input, width)
}

toolUseResult.stdout:
pub fn from_read_with_decorator<R, D>(input: R, width: usize, decorator: D) -> Result<String>
where
    R: io::Read,
    D: TextDecorator,
{
    config::with_decorator(decorator).string_from_read(input, width)
--
pub fn from_read<R>(input: R, width: usize) -> Result<String>
where
    R: io::Read,
{
    config::plain().string_from_read(input, width)
}
--
pub fn from_read_rich<R>(input: R, width: usize) -> Result<Vec<TaggedLine<Vec<RichAnnotation>>>>
where
    R: io::Read,
{
    config::rich().lines_from_read(input, width)
}

input.description: Check if PlainDecorator adds link footnotes
Check for footnote behavior in html2text

content.text:
Good — `include_link_footnotes` defaults to `false`. So `PlainDecorator` wraps link text in `[...]` but does NOT add footnote URLs. But `TrivialDecorator` doesn't even add brackets — it's the cleanest option.

Let me check if `config::plain_no_decorate()` exists and what it does.
Now let me check imports and existing tests.
Now I have the full picture. Let me apply all fixes:

content.content: The file /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/fix/html-to-text-use-crate/crates/tools/src/web_fetch.rs has been updated successfully.

lines: /// Convert HTML to plain text using the `html2text` crate.
/// Strips tags, decodes entities, collapses whitespace.
-/// Uses a proper HTML parser — safe for any encoding/multibyte content.
+/// Uses `TrivialDecorator` so link URLs and markup annotations are dropped.
+            tracing::warn!("html2text parse failed, returning raw body: {e}");

content.text: Now add a test for link hrefs not appearing in output:

content.content: The file /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/fix/html-to-text-use-crate/crates/tools/src/web_fetch.rs has been updated successfully.

lines:
+ let html = r#"

Visit our site today.

"#;
+        assert!(text.contains("our site"), "link text should be kept");
+            "link href must not appear in plain-text output"

content.text: Now let's run the tests to verify:

fix(tools): address PR review for html_to_text

- Add test asserting link hrefs do not leak into plain-text output
